Md Arifuzzaman

Graduate Research Assistant

Email: arif@nevada.unr.edu

About Me

Hi, I’m Arif, a graduate research assistant at the High Performance Computing and Networking (HPCN) LAB, University of Nevada, Reno. I have completed a Masters in Applied Statistics from East West University and did my bachelor’s in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology. Before pursuing a doctoral degree, I have worked as a Data Engineer at Telenor Health and Software Engineer at iPay Systems Limited. I specialized in parallel and distributed computing and currently working on the throughput optimizations of high performance networks. I also have hands-on experience in applied machine learning and scientific computing.

:

Publications

  • Md Arifuzzaman, and Engin Arslan "Online Optimization of File Transfers in High-Speed Networks" IEEE/ACM Supercomputing (SC) 2021
  • Hemanta Sapkota, Md Arifuzzaman, and Engin Arslan "Sample Transfer Optimization with Adaptive Deep Neural Network" 2019 IEEE/ACM Innovating the Network for Data-Intensive Science (Slides|Paper)
